_meta.notes: First town SW from Caelum, on the coastal road toward the Coral Anchorage. Tide-locked — the town's bell tower sits on a stone pier that floods at high tide; the bells ring on the tide rising and the tide falling, twice a day. The bell-keepers know the tide times to the minute. The town is also the last stop before the tidal causeway out to the reef.

tagline: The bells ring on the tide. Twice a day, on time.
ambientPool: Sea air comes through every door; everyone here is faintly salt-cured.
The bells start their slow swing as the water reaches the second step of the tower.
A halfling Salt-keeper buys salt-cured fish from the harbor and disagrees politely about the price.
Children play 'who can stay on the pier longest' and lose ankle-deep before they leave.

rooms.summary: A stone pier with the bell tower rising from its center. The pier floods at high tide; the bell-keepers wear sandals and don't mind. The bells are bronze, the rope is saltcord from Knot's Landing.
ambientPool: A bell-keeper of fifty-five steadies a rope and waits for the water to reach the step.
The bells sound: three slow strokes, a pause, three slow strokes. The town adjusts.
A child of six rings a small practice-bell in the doorway of the keeper's hut.
The tide is on time. The keepers smile thinly — it's their work for the world to do that.

exits.narrative: Down the road to the head of the tidal causeway out to the reef.

rooms.summary: Where the tidal causeway begins. A stone marker shows the safe-cross hours, updated daily by the bell-keepers. Travelers wait here for low tide; impatient travelers do not last long on this road.
ambientPool: A halfling Drowncaller stands at the marker and waits for an unfamiliar traveler to ask the right question.
The bell tolls in the distance; the marker is updated.
Two travelers from Knot's Landing accept they will not cross today and turn back to the inn.
The causeway itself is half-submerged. In an hour it will be half-emerged.

exits.narrative: Back along the road to the bell pier.
